title: Argentinian Seitan Empanadas
description: Richly flavored empanadas filled with seitan, raspberries, and cheese, featuring a unique nigiri-shaping technique, perfect for a vegan-friendly meal prep option, with a funky cheese-pull effect when bitten into.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roll out the empanada dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out circles of dough using a cookie cutter or the rim of a glass.
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and saute the chopped onion and minced garlic until softened.
Add the seitan to the skillet and cook until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Stir in the cumin and paprika, and cook for 1 minute.
Fold the dough circles in half over the filling, forming a half-moon shape, and press the edges together to seal.
Use your fingers to shape the empanadas into nigiri-like shapes, with a flat bottom and a curved top.
Place the empanadas on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each empanada.
Bake the empanadas for 20-25 minutes, or until golden brown.
While the empanadas are baking, heat the vegan cheese shreds in a saucepan over low heat, stirring constantly, until melted and smooth.
Remove the empanadas from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes, then serve with the melted cheese and raspberries.
